Create a new report bundle - Creates a new report bundle.  type must be one of either compliance or vulnerability. In the case of compliance, you must specify at least one benchmark (with optional profile) or one custom check.  For a vulnerability report, the feed_id field is required to specify the vulnerability feed that should be used for vulnerability assessment. scanMethod should be client-based, network unauthenticated,  remote authenticated, or sbom. platform should be windows, linux, or osx. target lists computer groups by their ID. engine is optional and defaults to cis-cat. For the benchmarks specified, the profileId field is optional but recommended.  If not specified, the SCAP engine will usually default to running the first profile found in the benchmark. start_time, end_time, distribute_seconds, and issue_seconds are all optional fields that will be used in the corresponding fields of the action created to execute the report.  If no start time is provided, the report will be distributed to the selected endpoints and executed immediately.

bundle.description: The report bundle object that was created.

get.description: List report bundles - Retrieve the list of all reports known to Comply.

delete.description: Delete a report bundle - Mark a report bundle as deleted.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the report bundle.

get.description: Get report publish job - Return JSON representation of an asynchronous publishing job.  Typically this endpoint is polled to check for the completion of a publishing job.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: The hash of the report bundle.

post.description: Publish a report - Publish a report bundle.  This creates the report zip bundle, attaches it to a Tanium package, and creates all Tanium collateral associated with a report bundle.  This endpoint returns a JSON object detailing the asynchronous job created to drive the publishing process.  Use the corresponding GET method to poll this job for progress of the publishing process.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: The hash of the report bundle.

parameters.name: isUpdate
parameters.description: (boolean) Preserves next start time if this bundle has a repeating schedule

job.description: Details of the asynchronous job created to drive report publishing.

executionPriority.description: The execution priority for the process that performs the assessment scan on the endpoint.

scanEngine.description: The scan engine of the report bundle.

distribute_seconds.description: The distribution time for the bundle report.

start_time.description: When the report bundle should start running.

end_time.description: When the report bundle should stop running.

issue_seconds.description: The number of seconds the assessment should be distributed over.

max_age_seconds.description: The max age of the bundle report.

scanNetworkDrives.description: If the bundle report should scan network drives.

includeNewEndpointsOnScan.description: If new endpoints should be scanned on subsequent scans. Best practice is to set this to false.

verifySshHostKeys.description: If the assessment should use public key comparisons to verify endpoints.

prioritizedCredentials.description: The list of credentials to scan with, in priority order.

post.description: Upload engine - Upload a new engine to Comply.  In the case of CIS-CAT and SCC, you need only supply the file upload and the importBenchmarks/importEngine flags.  The remainder of the file details will be determined by computing the hash of the package and looking up the details from Comply's library of accepted engines.  For JRE uploads, the version, platform, and architecture flags must be set.

parameters.name: engineName
parameters.description: One of cis-cat, scc, or joval.

parameters.name: importBenchmarks
parameters.description: true or false setting to import the benchmarks contained in the package.  Applies only to CIS-CAT and SCC packages.

parameters.name: importEngine
parameters.description: true or false setting to import the engine contained within a package.  This would be set to false in a situation where the user wanted to bulk import the benchmarks found within a package, but does not wish to import the engine itself.

parameters.name: forceImport
parameters.description: true or false setting to force the import.

job.description: Details of asynchronous job created to perform engine import.

license.description: File attachment of the license for the engine
